===============================================

Today is February 12, 2026. US President Donald Trump has reversed the 2009 "endangerment finding" regarding greenhouse gases. According to White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt, how much is this reversal expected to reduce spending for automobile manufacturers per vehicle?
(A) $2,400
(B) $1,400
(C) $2,500
(D) $1,000
(E) $2,100
explanation: The article explicitly states that White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt told reporters that reversing the finding would "reduce automobile manufacturers' spending by $2,400 per vehicle."
model_output:
White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt announced that the reversal of the 2009 "endangerment finding" is expected to reduce automobile manufacturers' spending by more than $2,400 per vehicle. ([kxxv.com](https://www.kxxv.com/politics/the-president/trump-administration-will-repeal-major-greenhouse-gas-regulations-this-week?utm_source=openai))

This action is part of a broader deregulatory effort by the Trump administration, aiming to eliminate federal greenhouse gas standards for automakers. The administration argues that these regulations have imposed significant costs on the automotive industry and consumers. ([nationaltoday.com](https://nationaltoday.com/us/dc/washington/news/2026/02/11/white-house-says-trump-will-revoke-climate-finding-underpinning-auto-emissions-rules?utm_source=openai))

Environmental groups have expressed concern that this rollback could lead to increased greenhouse gas emissions and air pollution, potentially offsetting any short-term economic benefits. ([arabnews.com](https://www.arabnews.com/node/2632556/amp?utm_source=openai))

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

Today is February 12, 2026. Regarding the conclusion of Operation Metro Surge in Minnesota, how many undocumented immigrants did Tom Homan state had been arrested during the operation?
(A) Roughly 800,000
(B) More than 4,000
(C) Approximately 1.6 million
(D) Exactly 5,000
(E) Just under 2,500
explanation: Tom Homan is quoted in the article stating that "More than 4,000 undocumented immigrants had been arrested" during the surge.
model_output:
Tom Homan, the White House border czar, announced that Operation Metro Surge in Minnesota resulted in over 4,000 arrests. ([cbsnews.com](https://www.cbsnews.com/minnesota/news/tom-homan-news-conference-minneapolis-feb-12/?utm_source=openai)) He emphasized that these arrests targeted individuals posing public safety threats, including murderers, sex offenders, gang members, and other violent criminals. ([breitbart.com](https://www.breitbart.com/crime/2026/02/12/tom-homan-ends-metro-surge-in-minneapolis/?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is (B) More than 4,000.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

(A) $1.4 million
(B) $865,000
(C) $1.2 million
(D) $2.4 million
(E) $1.5 million
explanation: The article states that the GoFundMe page "had raised $1.2m (£865,000) by Thursday morning."
model_output:
As of Thursday morning, February 12, 2026, the GoFundMe page established to support the family of actor James Van Der Beek had raised over $1 million in less than 24 hours. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/12/james-van-der-beek-fundraiser-wife-kimberly-children?utm_source=openai)) The exact amount raised at that time varies across sources, with reports indicating totals ranging from $1.2 million to $1.3 million. ([tmz.com](https://www.tmz.com/2026/02/12/james-van-der-beek-gofundme-raises-1-million-death/?utm_source=openai)) Given these variations, the most accurate answer based on the provided options is:

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 12, 2026. During the congressional hearing on the Jeffrey Epstein files, to what type of facility was Ghislaine Maxwell recently moved following a meeting with Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche?
(A) A maximum security federal penitentiary
(B) A low-security Florida state prison
(C) A high-security facility in New York
(D) A minimum security all-women Texas prison camp
(E) A medium-security detention center in Arizona
explanation: The article specifies that Ghislaine Maxwell was moved from a "low-security" Florida prison to a "minimum security" all-women Texas prison camp.
model_output:
After meeting with Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche in July 2025, Ghislaine Maxwell was transferred from a low-security federal correctional institution in Florida to a minimum-security federal prison camp in Texas. This transfer occurred shortly after their meeting, which was part of the Trump administration's effort to address public scrutiny over its handling of documents related to Jeffrey Epstein. ([yahoo.com](https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/ghislaine-maxwell-moved-lower-security-185536140.html?utm_source=openai))

The minimum-security federal prison camp in Texas is known for its lower level of security, featuring limited or no perimeter fencing, dormitory-style housing, and a relatively low staff-to-inmate ratio. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ghislaine_Maxwell?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 12, 2026. A collision occurred near South America between two US Navy vessels during a replenishment-at-sea operation. Which specific ships were involved in the incident?
(A) USS Truxtun and USNS Supply
(B) USS Abraham Lincoln and USNS Supply
(C) USS Truxtun and USS Supply
(D) USS Arleigh Burke and USNS Caribbean
(E) USS Truxtun and USNS Kanawha
explanation: The article identifies the vessels as the "Arleigh Burke-class destroyer USS Truxtun and the Supply-class fast combat support ship USNS Supply."
model_output:
On February 11, 2026, during a replenishment-at-sea operation in the Caribbean Sea, the U.S. Navy destroyer USS Truxtun (DDG-103) collided with the supply ship USNS Supply (T-AOE-6). The incident resulted in minor injuries to two personnel, both of whom are in stable condition. Both ships have reported sailing safely, and the collision is under investigation. ([stripes.com](https://www.stripes.com/branches/navy/2026-02-12/navy-ships-collide-south-america-20713967.html?utm_source=openai))

Given the options provided, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

Today is February 12, 2026. The US House of Representatives recently voted on a resolution to rescind President Trump's tariffs on Canadian goods. What was the final vote count for this resolution?
(A) 211 to 219
(B) 219 to 211
(C) 225 to 205
(D) 218 to 212
(E) 220 to 210
explanation: The article states that the resolution passed in a "219 to 211 vote."
model_output:
The U.S. House of Representatives recently voted to rescind President Trump's tariffs on Canadian goods. The final vote count was 219 to 211, with six Republicans joining Democrats in support of the resolution. ([cbsnews.com](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/house-votes-trump-canada-tariffs/?utm_source=openai))

This vote represents a rare bipartisan rebuke of the president's trade policy, as the resolution passed with support from both parties. However, the measure is largely symbolic, as it would require Senate approval and the president's signature to take effect. ([cfr.org](https://www.cfr.org/articles/the-house-votes-to-rein-in-trumps-canada-tariffs/?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is (B) 219 to 211.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

([jweekly.com](https://jweekly.com/2026/02/11/after-3-hour-white-house-meeting-trump-says-he-insisted-to-netanyahu-that-iran-talks-should-continue/?utm_source=openai))

===============================================

Today is February 12, 2026. The airspace over El Paso, Texas, was recently closed, causing significant disruption. What was the specific radius of the flight restriction area published by the FAA?
(A) 5-mile radius
(B) 15-mile radius
(C) 20-mile radius
(D) 10-mile radius
(E) 25-mile radius
explanation: The article states: "The restriction covered a 10-mile radius around El Paso, including parts of southern New Mexico."
model_output:
The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) issued a temporary flight restriction over El Paso, Texas, effective from February 10 at 11:30 PM MST to February 20 at 11:30 PM MST. This restriction prohibited all aircraft operations within a 10-nautical-mile radius of El Paso International Airport, extending from the ground up to 17,999 feet. The FAA classified this airspace as "National Defense Airspace," warning that aircraft violating the restriction could be intercepted, detained, and interviewed by law enforcement or security personnel. ([abc13.com](https://abc13.com/18584655/?utm_source=openai))

The restriction was lifted on February 12, 2026, after the FAA stated that the threat had been neutralized. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/El_Paso_International_Airport?utm_source=openai))

Given the options provided, the correct answer is (D) 10-mile radius.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 12, 2026. Bad Bunny recently headlined the Super Bowl half-time show at Levi's Stadium. According to the report, how many viewers watched the performance?
(A) 125 million
(B) 100 million
(C) 150 million
(D) 115 million
(E) 135 million
explanation: The article states that Bad Bunny "performed on Sunday almost entirely in Spanish at Santa Clara's Levi's Stadium for an audience of 125 million viewers."
model_output:
Bad Bunny's Super Bowl LX halftime show attracted an average of 128.2 million U.S. viewers from 8:15 to 8:30 p.m. Eastern Time, according to Nielsen's ratings. ([parade.com](https://parade.com/entertainment/bad-bunny-super-bowl-halftime-viewership/?utm_source=openai)) This performance ranks as the fourth most-watched halftime show in history, following Kendrick Lamar's 2025 show with 133.5 million viewers, Michael Jackson's 1993 performance with 133.4 million viewers, and Usher's 2024 show with 129.3 million viewers. ([chicago.suntimes.com](https://chicago.suntimes.com/sports-media/2026/02/10/super-bowl-lx-averages-124-9-million-viewers-bad-bunny-gets-128-2-million-for-halftime-show?utm_source=openai))

Given the options provided, the closest figure to the reported viewership is 125 million.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 12, 2026. The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) originally issued the "endangerment finding" in 2009. How many specific planet-warming greenhouse gases were identified in that ruling as a danger to human health?
(A) Four
(B) Six
(C) Five
(D) Eight
(E) Seven
explanation: The article states: "The agency decided that six key planet-warming greenhouse gases, including carbon dioxide and methane, were a danger to human health."
model_output:
In 2009, the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) issued an "endangerment finding" under the Clean Air Act, determining that six greenhouse gases pose a threat to public health and welfare. These gases are:

- Carbon dioxide (CO₂)
- Methane (CH₄)
- Nitrous oxide (N₂O)
- Hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs)
- Perfluorocarbons (PFCs)
- Sulfur hexafluoride (SF₆)

Therefore, the correct answer is (B) Six.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 12, 2026. Long-term airspace closures are rare in the US. According to the FAA website, since which year has the agency cut average airspace closure times from over four hours to just over two hours per launch?
(A) 2015
(B) 2020
(C) 2019
(D) 2021
(E) 2018
explanation: The article states: "Since 2018, the FAA has cut airspace closures from an average of more than four hours per launch to just more than two hours, according to its web site."
model_output:
The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has implemented various measures to enhance the efficiency of airspace management during space operations. Prior to 2014, space operations were relatively infrequent, and the FAA accommodated them on an as-needed basis by closing large volumes of airspace for extended periods. However, as the frequency of space launches and reentries increased, the FAA adopted new technologies and operating procedures to improve airspace management. Since 2018, these efforts have successfully reduced the average duration of airspace closures from over four hours to just over two hours per launch. ([faa.gov](https://www.faa.gov/space/airspace_integration?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is (E) 2018.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

(D) 13:45
(E) 13:30
explanation: The article states: "The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) said they received a report of an active shooter at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School at 13:30 local time on Tuesday (20:30 GMT)."
model_output:
The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) received a report of an active shooter at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School at approximately 1:20 p.m. Pacific Standard Time (PST) on February 10, 2026. ([rcmp.ca](https://rcmp.ca/en/bc/tumbler-ridge/news/2026/02/4350208?utm_source=openai)) Since Tumbler Ridge operates on Mountain Standard Time (MST), which is one hour ahead of PST, the local time of the report was 2:20 p.m.

Given the options provided, the closest match to this time is (B) 14:00.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>
